The standard deviation (SD) is the standard error multiplied by the square root of the sample size (n = 100), giving us an SD of 1 g/dL. The z-score is (18 - 17) / 1 = 1. Therefore, we can look up this z-score in the standard normal distribution table, which indicates that approximately 84% of the population has a hemoglobin concentration below 18 g/dL. This implies that about 16% have a hemoglobin concentration greater than 18 g/dL, making the closest option 5%.
